payload.description: A worn stone archway marks the edge of the market. The stones are smooth from centuries of hands brushing them for luck on the way in — a tradition that predates any living vendor and which nobody has ever verified actually works. Beyond the arch, the noise hits you. A heavy brass turnstile blocks the path north, currently as immovable as the stones themselves. Nearby, a heap of junk has accumulated against the wall. A faded sign bolted to the archway reads TINKERMARKET in letters made from dozens of different metals.

payload.name: stuck turnstile
payload.description: A heavy brass turnstile that guards the entrance to the market proper. It looks like it should rotate, but the socket for the operating lever is empty. This is apparently the market's way of ensuring only people with basic mechanical skills or a lot of patience get inside.
payload.secret: The turnstile is part of a series of security measures that haven't been maintained since the last Great Reorganization. It requires a simple lever to function. Any long, sturdy rod will do, but the one in the junk pile is the most convenient.

payload.name: heap of junk
payload.description: A heap of discarded dimensional shipping crates and bent metal. It's the kind of pile that accumulates when nobody is quite sure if something is trash or a rare component. Someone has thoughtfully added a sign that says NOT FOR SCAVENGING, which has been largely ignored. A rusty lever pokes out from the mess.
payload.secret: The pile contains a few useful bits of scrap. This is a common occurrence at the market — one person's junk is another's critical spare part.

payload.name: rusty lever
payload.description: A solid iron bar with a handle at one end. It looks like it was once part of a larger machine. It's heavy, rusted, and exactly the kind of thing you'd want if you needed to move something very stubborn.
payload.secret: This lever was originally the manual override for a pressure valve in the Rendering Yards. It's now perfectly sized to fit the socket of the gate turnstile. It can also be used as a crude club, though the market has rules against that sort of thing.

payload.name: Kip
payload.description: An elderly vendor with skin like parchment who sits on a stool by the gate, watching the traffic with an expression of profound boredom. He wears a vest with too many pockets and a hat that might have once been a different color. He seems to be the unofficial gatekeeper, or at least the person most likely to complain if you do something wrong.
payload.secret: Kip has been sitting here for forty years. He doesn't work for the market; he just likes to watch the people. He knows the turnstile is broken and enjoys watching newcomers figure it out. If asked, he'll mention that 'the last fellow who tried to force it ended up with a very sore shoulder and a very intact turnstile.'

payload.perform: const exit = world.getEntity('exit:gate:north'); const turnstile = world.getEntity('item:stuck-turnstile'); const lever = world.getEntity('item:rusty-lever'); exit.properties.locked = false; turnstile.description = 'The brass turnstile is now functional, thanks to the rusty lever you have jammed into the socket. It rotates with a satisfyingly heavy click.'; lever.location = 'item:stuck-turnstile'; lever.tags = ['fixed']; return 'With a bit of force, you slide the rusty lever into the turnstile socket. It fits perfectly. You give it a push, and the mechanism groans before clicking into its first rotation. The way north is clear.';

priorState.name: stuck turnstile
priorState.description: A heavy brass turnstile that guards the entrance to the market proper. It looks like it should rotate, but the socket for the operating lever is empty. This is apparently the market's way of ensuring only people with basic mechanical skills or a lot of patience get inside.
priorState.secret: The turnstile is part of a series of security measures that haven't been maintained since the last Great Reorganization. It requires a simple lever to function. Any long, sturdy rod will do, but the one in the junk pile is the most convenient.

payload.name: turnstile with lever
payload.description: A heavy brass turnstile with a rusty lever shoved into its mechanism. It looks like it might actually move now, provided you don't mind the sound of grinding metal. It has been here for as long as anyone can remember, which in at least one vendor's case is eleven minutes.

payload.description: A worn stone archway marks the edge of the market. The stones are smooth from centuries of hands brushing them for luck on the way in — a tradition that predates any living vendor and which nobody has ever verified actually works. Beyond the arch, the noise hits you. A heavy brass turnstile, once immovable, now stands rotated to one side. It moves with a sound like a choir of saws. Nearby, a heap of junk has accumulated against the wall. A faded sign bolted to the archway reads TINKERMARKET in letters made from dozens of different metals.

payload.name: rotated turnstile
payload.description: The brass turnstile is now rotated, clearing the way north. It groans occasionally, as if offended by the sudden movement.

payload.name: rusty lever
payload.description: A long, heavy bar of iron with a grip at one end. It’s pitted with rust but still feels sturdy enough to move a mountain, or at least a very stubborn piece of machinery.
payload.secret: This lever was once part of the gate's original mechanism. It's the only thing in this pile that actually fits the turnstile's socket. It smells faintly of old grease and ozone.

payload.name: stuck turnstile
payload.description: A heavy brass turnstile blocks the path north. Its gears are visible through a gap in the casing, and there's a distinct socket where a handle or lever should be. It looks like it hasn't moved since the last era.

payload.name: Hidden Storage
payload.description: A void where items not yet found are stored.

payload.name: brass turnstile
payload.description: A heavy brass turnstile blocks the path north. It looks like it was designed to be operated with a long handle, but the socket where one would go is currently empty. Cold air whistles through the mechanism.

A heap of discarded dimensional shipping crates and bent metal. It's the kind of pile that accumulates when nobody is quite sure if something is trash or a rare component. A rusty iron lever pokes out from the mess, wedged between two heavy crates. Someone has thoughtfully added a sign that says NOT FOR SCAVENGING, which has been largely ignored.

payload.perform: const lever = store.get('item:rusty-lever'); if (lever.location === player.id) return { output: 'You already have the lever.', events: [] }; lever.location = player.id; return { output: 'You brace your feet against a heavy crate and heave. With a screech of protesting metal, the rusty lever slides out of the junk pile. It\'s heavy, cold, and smells of old grease.', events: [{ type: 'set-property', entityId: 'item:rusty-lever', property: 'location', value: player.id, description: 'Took the lever from the junk pile.' }] };

if (indirect.id !== 'item:stuck-turnstile') return { output: 'That doesn\'t seem to fit there.', events: [] }; object.location = 'room:gate-hidden-storage'; indirect.properties.switchedOn = true; indirect.name = 'turnstile (with lever)'; indirect.description = 'A heavy brass turnstile blocks the path north. An iron lever has been slotted into the socket, providing the necessary leverage to turn the gears.'; return { output: 'You slide the iron lever into the turnstile\'s socket. It fits perfectly, with a satisfying metallic clunk.', events: [ { type: 'set-property', entityId: object.id, property: 'location', value: 'room:gate-hidden-storage', description: 'Used the lever.' }, { type: 'set-property', entityId: indirect.id, property: 'switchedOn', value: true, description: 'Lever inserted (switchedOn:true).' } ] };

if (!object.properties.switchedOn) return { output: 'You push against the brass arms, but without a lever for mechanical advantage, they won\'t budge. Your shoulder hurts just thinking about it.', events: [] }; const exit = store.get('exit:gate:north'); exit.properties.locked = false; return { output: 'You lean your weight into the iron lever. The ancient gears within the turnstile groan, then suddenly give way with a series of heavy clicks. The way north is now clear.', events: [{ type: 'set-property', entityId: 'exit:gate:north', property: 'locked', value: false, description: 'Unlocked the path north.' }] };

scenery.description: The lever is a pitted, iron-colored bar that terminates in a heavy ball of lead. It is wedged deep into the pile, connected to a mechanism that suggests it was once part of a high-pressure valve or a very large jack. It feels unnervingly solid for something that has been thrown away.
scenery.rejection: The lever is jammed tight against several heavy crates; it won't budge without clearing the junk first.

The gears inside the turnstile shift, ready to move.', events: [{ type: 'set-property', entityId: 'exit:gate:north', property: 'locked', value: false, description: 'Unlocked the turnstile.' }, { type: 'set-property', entityId: 'item:stuck-turnstile', property: 'name', value: 'brass turnstile (with lever)', description: 'Updated turnstile name.' }, { type: 'set-property', entityId: 'item:stuck-turnstile', property: 'description', value: 'The heavy brass turnstile now has a rusty iron lever slotted into its socket.', description: 'Updated turnstile description.' }, { type: 'set-property', entityId: 'item:rusty-lever', property: 'location', value: 'item:stuck-turnstile', description: 'Moved lever into turnstile.' }] };

const turnstile = object; if (turnstile.id !== 'item:stuck-turnstile') { return { output: 'You can\'t turn that.', events: [] }; } const exit = store.get('exit:gate:north'); if (exit.properties.locked) { return { output: 'The turnstile is locked tight. It needs a lever to rotate the gears.', events: [] }; } return { output: 'You push against the turnstile. It rotates with a satisfyingly heavy series of clicks, allowing you to pass through.', events: [] };

scenery.description: The lever is a pitted, iron-colored bar that terminates in a heavy ball of lead. It is wedged deep into the pile, connected to a mechanism that suggests it was once part of a high-pressure valve or a very large jack. It feels unnervingly solid for something that has been thrown away.
scenery.rejection: The lever is jammed tight against several heavy crates; it won't budge without clearing the junk first.

payload.name: brass turnstile
payload.description: A heavy brass turnstile blocks the path north. It's missing its operating lever, leaving only an empty, grease-slicked socket where a handle should be. A few years of grime have settled into the gears.

payload.name: rusty lever
payload.description: A pitted, iron-colored bar that terminates in a heavy ball of lead. It feels unnervingly solid and smells faintly of old grease. This was clearly meant to move something heavy.
payload.secret: This lever fits perfectly into the brass turnstile's socket. Its weight suggests it was forged for high-torque applications.

priorState.name: heap of junk
priorState.description: A heap of discarded dimensional shipping crates and bent metal. It's the kind of pile that accumulates when nobody is quite sure if something is trash or a rare component. Someone has thoughtfully added a sign that says NOT FOR SCAVENGING, which has been largely ignored. A rusty lever pokes out from the mess.
priorState.secret: The pile contains a few useful bits of scrap. This is a common occurrence at the market — one person's junk is another's critical spare part.

scenery.description: The lever is a pitted, iron-colored bar that terminates in a heavy ball of lead. It is wedged deep into the pile, connected to a mechanism that suggests it was once part of a high-pressure valve or a very large jack. It feels unnervingly solid for something that has been thrown away.
scenery.rejection: The lever is jammed tight against several heavy crates; it won't budge without clearing the junk first.

priorState.name: rusty lever
priorState.description: A long, heavy bar of iron with a grip at one end. It’s pitted with rust but still feels sturdy enough to move a mountain, or at least a very stubborn piece of machinery.
priorState.secret: This lever was once part of the gate's original mechanism. It's the only thing in this pile that actually fits the turnstile's socket. It smells faintly of old grease and ozone.

payload.name: stuck turnstile
payload.description: A heavy brass turnstile blocks the path north. It's missing its operating lever, leaving only an empty socket that looks like it's seen better centuries.

priorState.name: turnstile with lever
priorState.description: A heavy brass turnstile blocks the path north. An iron lever has been slotted into the socket, giving you the leverage needed to move the ancient gears.
priorState.secret: The turnstile is part of a series of security measures that haven't been maintained since the last Great Reorganization. It requires a simple lever to function. Any long, sturdy rod will do, but the one in the junk pile is the most convenient.
